sources.title: High Tide
sources.description: Title: High Tide Summary: A fast-moving automobile fails to make a sharp turn of the highway from Malibu to Los Angeles. It overturns and its occupants, Tim Slade, Newspaper man turned private investigator, and Hugh... Directed by: John Reinhardt Actors: Lee Tracy, Don Castle, Julie Bishop Production Company: Wrather Productions Release Date: 13 September 1947 (USA) Aspect Ratio: 1.37 : 1 A fast-moving automobile fails to make a sharp turn of the highway from Malibu to Los Angeles. It overturns and its occupants, Tim Slade, Newspaper man turned private investigator, and Hugh Fresney, managing editor of the Los Angeles Currier, are pinned underneath. Both are seriously injured and are unable to move. As they wait for the tide to come in and cover them, Fresney recounts that his boss, Clint VAughn has been killed, presumably by gangsters in the pay of Nick Dyke, who Fresney has fought against through his newspaper columns. Slade had been hired to avenge Fresney if Dyke's gang killed him in retaliation, Slade, one-time sweetheart of Vaughn's dipsomaniac wife Julie Vaughn, believed she still loved him, but he had fallen for Dana Jons, Vaughn's beautiful secretary. Police Detective O'Haffey investigated Vaughn's death and unearthed several suspects, including Slade, Julie, Dykes and Dana, but Slade was not satisfied and looked elsewhere.

sources.title: High Tide 1947 colorized (Don Castle, Lee Tracy)
sources.description: Private detective Tim Slade (Don Castle) is hired by an old friend, newspaper editor Hugh Fresney (Lee Tracy), to protect him from a gangster (Anthony Warde), about whom the editor is planning an exposé. When the newspaper's publisher Clint Vaughn (Douglas Walton) is shot, Slade comes under suspicion by Inspector O'Haffey (Regis Toomey) because of his past association with Vaughn's wife (Julie Bishop) and when he is found at the scene of a second murder. Slade gets help from Vaughn's attractive secretary (Anabel Shaw). The entire story is told in flashback after an accident leaves Slade and Fresney pinned under a car at the seashore, with the incoming high tide about to engulf them. Based on the story "Inside Job" by Raoul Whitfield. Lee Tracy as Hugh Fresney Don Castle as Tim Slade Julie Bishop as Julie Vaughn Anabel Shaw as Dana Jones Regis Toomey as Inspector O'Haffey Douglas Walton as Clinton Vaughn Francis Ford as Pop Garrow Anthony Warde as Nick Dyke I colorized this using AI software that doesn't always get the colors right, but I think it looks fairly natural. Hopefully a more professional job will be done someday. For questions or general comments, please use the Colorized Movies Forum at https://archive.org/details/colorized-movies?tab=forum

metadata.Plot: A fast-moving automobile fails to make a sharp turn of the highway from Malibu to Los Angeles. It overturns and its occupants, Tim Slade, Newspaper man turned private investigator, and Hugh Fresney, managing editor of the Los Angeles Currier, are pinned underneath. Both are seriously injured and are unable to move. As they wait for the tide to come in and cover them, Fresney recounts that his boss, Clint VAughn has been killed, presumably by gangsters in the pay of Nick Dyke, who Fresney has fought against through his newspaper columns. Slade had been hired to avenge Fresney if Dyke's gang killed him in retaliation, Slade, one-time sweetheart of Vaughn's dipsomaniac wife Julie Vaughn, believed she still loved him, but he had fallen for Dana Jons, Vaughn's beautiful secretary. Police Detective O'Haffey investigated Vaughn's death and unearthed several suspects, including Slade, Julie, Dykes and Dana, but Slade was not satisfied and looked elsewhere.
